compiled_state.title: Update project docs to reflect severity-only review schema
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Update CLAUDE.md architecture section and any other documentation that references the old review schema (scores, REVIEW_RESULT, MIN_SCORE).
**Why**: Documentation must accurately reflect the new severity-only schema so future agents and developers have correct awareness.
**Scope**:
- IN: CLAUDE.md (tiered review description, review dimensions reference, review-status file format), any other docs referencing scores or REVIEW_RESULT
- OUT: Code changes (Stories 1-3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md accurately describes the severity-only review schema and does not reference numeric scores, REVIEW_RESULT, or MIN_SCORE
  ← Satisfies: All success criteria (documentation accuracy)
- When this story is complete, the review-status file format documented in CLAUDE.md matches the actual format (no score= line)
  ← Satisfies: "The review-status file no longer contains a score= line"

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for formatting and conventions

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for documentation formatting, structure, and conventions.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Consideration: [Scope Boundary] plan-review/SKILL.md (lines 85, 90, 113) references scores (feasibility, completeness, yagni, codebase_alignment) for the PLAN review schema, not CODE review. When grepping for 'scores' across .md files, do NOT modify plan-review scoring — it is a separate system. Evaluate each match for which review system it belongs to.
comments.author: Test
